title: Vegetable Poha
description: Light, tangy flattened-rice breakfast with peanuts, turmeric, and everyday vegetables.
explanation: Poha is a classic Indian breakfast that cooks fast, stays light, and takes well to whatever vegetables you have. Peanuts add crunch and a little protein; lemon and coriander finish it bright.
Rinse the poha just enough to soften it—over-soaking makes it mushy. Medium-thick (medium) poha is the most forgiving.
instructions: Place poha in a colander, rinse under water, drain, and fluff with a fork. Sprinkle salt and turmeric; set aside.
Heat oil. Add mustard seeds, curry leaves, chilli, and peanuts. Saute until peanuts are fragrant.
Add onion and potato if using; cook until potato is tender.
Add the softened poha and mix gently on low heat for 2–3 minutes.
Turn off heat, squeeze lemon, add coriander, and serve.
stepNotes: Poha should feel soft but separate after rinsing—not wet and clumpy.
Skip onion/potato for a faster Jain-friendly version with peas and grated carrot.
Roast raw peanuts in the tempering if you do not have pre-roasted ones.
Keep heat low once poha is in so it does not dry out or break.
Lemon at the end keeps the flavor fresh.

variationSections.title: Regional twists
items: Indori style: add sev and a pinch of sugar.
Maharashtrian style: more lemon, green chilli, and coriander.
South-leaning: add grated coconut and a pinch of hing.

variationSections.title: Make it more filling
items: Add boiled green peas or crumbled tofu.
Top with sprouts for extra protein.
Serve with a glass of soy milk or plant curd on the side.
